old_claim.note: Basic Hunting & Gathering is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Basic Hunting & Gathering supplies the forager subsistence context in which reading animal tracks, trails, and movements is useful, but it is not a staged predecessor invention.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and ecology sections describe hunting and gathering as the oldest human mode of making a living; the gender/comparisons section says many hunts would not be successful without keen observations of animal tracks and movements.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Hunter-gatherer hunting practice includes keen observations of animal tracks and movements.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports animal tracking as part of forager hunting ecology, which is contextual infrastructure rather than a separate historical stage.

ontology_before: The graph treated basic hunting and gathering as a predecessor stage leading to animal tracking.
ontology_after: Animal tracking is scoped as an interpretive skill within forager hunting ecology, with broad hunting and gathering modeled as contextual common dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hunting_gathering_basic remains connected but is retyped from historical_predecessor to common_dependency.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a later rock-art-only representation of tracks.
A source showed animal tracking should be modeled as a separate later invention after basic hunting and gathering.
The hunting_gathering_basic node were narrowed away from broad forager subsistence.
short_reason: Tracking is part of hunter-gatherer hunting ecology, not a staged successor to it.
